working_on: Engine apparatus: close the routine close-out gap surfaced at S-0072 boot — every routine fire was needing boot-freshness FF to catch up to origin/main. Three structural fixes (parent main FF post-push in routine_lifecycle_push.py; new routine_worktree_sweep.py at routine close; ADR 0054 amendment + ops-doc + Skill body sync) plus three batched cleanups (S-0071 archive missing_rigor_score 289 → 316 per Issue #25; recover S-0057 + S-0067 archive close-out content from stranded worktree state; one-shot Issue #16 worktree cleanup including 3 remote-only branch deletes with user authorization).
declared_scope: engine/tools/routine_lifecycle_push.py (extend with parent_ff helper + post-push wiring), engine/tools/test_routine_lifecycle_push.py (7 new pytests), engine/tools/routine_worktree_sweep.py (new tool), engine/tools/test_routine_worktree_sweep.py (new test file, 18 pytests), engine/operations/routine-mode-operations.md + .claude/skills/routine-mode-lifecycle/SKILL.md (Layer 1 doc + Skill body), engine/adr/0054-...md (Consequences amendment), engine/session/archive/S-0071.json (Issue #25 fix), engine/session/archive/S-0057.json + engine/session/archive/S-0067.json (recover stranded close-out content), engine/STATE.md + engine/ENGINE_LOG.md (close prose). phase: NA-engine-apparatus
outcome_summary: Routine close-out apparatus fix lands at S-0072. Six commits: eager-claim 2495a38 (claim S-0072 with full context); feat 9ed7bfd (parent FF post-push in routine_lifecycle_push.py for all three modes — eager-claim, deliverable, close — best-effort, failure logged but not propagated, boot-freshness remains safety net per ADR 0052; closes the asymmetry vs interactive close where parent main was being left stale, observable as the 'merge refs/remotes/origin/main: Fast-forward' boot-freshness recovery shape replacing the interactive close's 'merge claude/<branch>: Fast-forward' shape); feat 1dd2449 (routine_worktree_sweep.py — new tool invoked at routine close last-action after lock release; chdirs to parent BEFORE git worktree remove for macOS fork-safety; pre-flight checks mirror sweep_worktrees.sh exactly; closes Issue #16 follow-on); docs 6459a96 (Layer 1 ops doc + Skill body + ADR 0054 Amendment all sync per ADR 0044 doc → skill flow); chore 5a0e03b (Issue #25 fix — S-0071 archive missing_rigor_score 289 → 316; +27 delta corresponds to 28 newly-seeded P5-08 nodes lacking topology data for the rigor formula); chore 1031f2d (recover S-0057 + S-0067 archive close-out content from stranded worktree state — both archives were stuck on origin/main in partial-close shape because the close push didn't propagate the archive write-back from amazing-mayer-f501e9 / heuristic-gates-d71b84 worktrees; the same close-out gap pattern the new wrapper-side parent FF closes structurally going forward; clears the two known instances directly). Test coverage: 7 new pytests in test_routine_lifecycle_push.py (32 total) for parent FF; 18 new pytests in new test_routine_worktree_sweep.py. validate.py 30 checks, 0 hard-fails, 325 soft-warns (316 missing_rigor_score continuing partial-seed pattern unchanged from S-0071 since this apparatus session didn't touch the seed graph; 8 issue_collision down from 9 because Issue #16 + #25 closed; 1 empty_declared_scope which this archive resolves). Worktree cleanup completed in-session per user policy (no manual processes, all auditable here): 12 stale claude/* worktrees + branches swept (9 in batch from sweep_worktrees.sh dry-run set; 3 after recovering close-out content from amazing-mayer S-0057 + heuristic-gates S-0067 + discarding flamboyant's trivial validate-history.jsonl test artifact); 3 remote-only claude/* branches deleted with user-authorized destructive action (claude/sharp-clarke-93896d, claude/zen-khorana-89af0c, claude/probe-push-gate-20260505T045706Z — Phase 0 wrapper-bypass probe artifact, T1-A long-confirmed). Origin now carries 0 claude/* refs (down from 3); local repo carries 1 worktree (this session) + 1 branch (down from 14 / 13). 0 in-band Issues filed; 0 HANDOFF entries. Issues closed: #16 (worktree buildup main action + the auto-clean follow-on the issue named as separate-Issue-if-pursued is now landed); #25 (S-0071 archive missing_rigor_score). Issues remaining open: #1 (upstream MemPalace), #2 (upstream MemPalace), #20 (archive-history audit pass — separate follow-on; the recovery here clears the two known instances directly but the audit mechanism remains), #21 (telemetry None pattern), #22 (Metabase eval), #23 (apply_migration postcondition verification), #24 (graph renderer). **Boot-freshness behavior at next routine fire is the load-bearing observation**: routine_boot_freshness.py should exit silently against a fresh worktree because parent main is now FF'd at every push. The first observable confirmation will be the next routine session's archived outcome_summary not naming 'boot freshness intervention' or 'fast-forwarded N commit(s)' surface text. Forward state: phase 5 unchanged at 12/16 complete; next routine fire (S-0073) picks P5-09 Philosophy of science in walk order. The original /start-engine eager-claim was preceded by a manual recovery push attempt (`git push origin 9e3d89d:main`) of S-0071's close commit which returned 'Everything up-to-date' — origin/main already had S-0071's close because the routine completed normally during plan authoring; the user explicitly named that S-0071 was actively running when this conversation began.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes. Plan declared scope was the four-fix bundle (parent FF, auto-sweep, Issue #16 cleanup, Issue #25 archive correction) plus the planned files-to-modify list. All landed: parent_ff() in routine_lifecycle_push.py (commit 9ed7bfd), routine_worktree_sweep.py (commit 1dd2449), routine-mode-operations.md + SKILL.md + ADR 0054 amendment (commit 6459a96), S-0071 archive correction (commit 5a0e03b), Issue #16 worktree cleanup (executed in-session not as a commit, plus remote-branch deletes user-authorized in-session). Two pieces emerged mid-session that weren't in the plan but were directly aligned with the same close-out gap: (a) recovery of S-0057 + S-0067 archive close-out content from stranded worktree state (commit 1031f2d) — exactly the pattern the new wrapper-side parent FF prevents structurally going forward, surfaced when investigating the 3 dirty worktrees the sweep_worktrees.sh dry-run flagged; (b) deleting the 3 remote-only claude/* branches with user authorization (the plan held only probe-push-gate explicitly out-of-scope; user authorized in-session). Both are properly close-out-gap-fix scope, not session bloat. No descoping; the plan's deferred items remain deferred (ADR 0052 boot-freshness is still load-bearing as defense; Issue #20 archive-history audit remains a separate follow-on; Issue #21 scan_context_telemetry None pattern is a separate session).
